pages.notes: Page is dominated by glued-in photos of Caerleon: top photo shows Roman soldiers re-enactment in a field; middle-left shows a classical building (museum or temple front); middle-right shows an aerial view of the excavated Roman amphitheatre; bottom shows a large stone carved face/head. Caption at bottom reads: Caerleon "Fort of the Legion". No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
April 13th 2018
 Simon, Eve & Tinder left for Wales to visit John & Kagin their new house (11am). We stopped in Bourneville to buy buttons.
      We parked by the river park, flat land, below Ross on Wye & walked up the hill for a good lunch at Man of Ross. We bought crafts & books.
      On to Caerleon ("fort of the legion"). The amphitheatre (could seat 6,000) was excavated 1927, but the rest – granary, barracks, has only been excavated/being excavated since the '60. An interesting museum – a mock barrack room for 8 men with bunks – nice curators.
      We spent the night at a campsite – "White House Farm"* a miles' walk away. Heater in modern shower block. V. Clean.
      We have forgotten marmalade & tea towels.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number in top-right corner reads '4' — likely a diary-internal page number rather than a volume page number. The name 'Kagin' could be 'Kayin' or another spelling — the handwriting is somewhat ambiguous. 'Bourneville' may be 'Bournville' (the Birmingham suburb) — the diary spelling is used verbatim. The '60 refers to the 1960s. The asterisk after 'White House Farm*' may indicate a footnote not visible on this page.
